textByHashes.82d175e8f7e6def252e0c50cec2ef136398aaf32: Hey, stay off my ship! That's private property!
textByHashes.fd0645c96b515cae0297cf6f7261b3fd4e2a8051: Hello there! Interested in buying this fine ship? I'm sure I can offer her to you for a much better price than any of those shipwrights ask for!
textByHashes.7b0f1f79efe1b1df1d59343675854e3290ef6f6a: Well, she's not quite seaworthy right now...
textByHashes.c37aee6219f415cf218bdf0b2bc3079d0c89392a: Because this isn't just any ship! This is the Lady Lumbridge, one of the last Crandorian ships! Despite her size, she's one of the most agile ship around!
textByHashes.e758e38bcc6c2ab433a355ccda63fd5a2f9a16ec: No, but a bit of work will quickly sort that out!
textByHashes.c40660d84511fc4d8600ebc2baaa32105cb4e3c8: Of course! I'm sure the work needed to do on it wouldn't be too expensive.
textByHashes.71f786c4fb3da092cad8e4109869ab797e0e098a: How does 2,000 gold sound? I'll even throw in my cabin boy, Jenkins, for free! He'll swab the decks and splice the mainsails for you!
textByHashes.a31f709a51fa530b93bbd7fc322ce60fbd1f9802: Oh, there was no particular accident, just years of wear and tear. It's been a long time since Crandor was destroyed and she's had several owners since then. Unfortunately, not all of them looked after her.
textByHashes.929d2fcc3756c09e2f96af3e85c14d42f6a674cf: Hello, captain! Here to inspect your new ship? Just a little work and she'll be seaworthy again.
textByHashes.8423ec9b344779c18ed1eea1c03fba278746b229: Well, the big gaping hole in the hold is the problem. You'll need a few wooden planks hammered in with steel nails to fix it.
textByHashes.c25bd166a4d37412676d82e72ef3ee229a3ac345: I dunno. It looks fine to me.
textByHashes.a2596f739d10616d04cf385f11fbbc9ede38a5f3: Yes, but that was before you fixed it!
textByHashes.1b59767dcd310632267e1d08832a5cfbb5dac989: Crandor's not something we sailors joke about. You can't sail from here to Catherby, or Entrana, or Ardougne without going past that accursed island.
textByHashes.6cddaf98a08384dfa3a034206e54586bf5a6e620: You can't get close to it because of the reefs, but you can always see it.
textByHashes.de8310670bf370656facc7b933666cc2983b7c7d: Sometimes you can see a dark shape in the sky, circling the island. That's when you have to sail on as quick as you can and pray it's not hungry. Every year, more ships are lost to that dragon.
textByHashes.bc25d6a689dcc9ae790e2cf73d1bbaaa309a2308: That island is surrounded by reefs that would rip this ship to shreds. Even if you found a map, you'd need an experienced captain to stand a chance of getting through
textByHashes.d13b70b0b194981f4280f1cfa459309f33ee1d24: and, even if I could get to it, there's no way I'm going any closer to that dragon than I have to. They say it can destroy whole ships with one bite.
textByHashes.39cce1befba67cd6ed3cdaa4bb6d8cb95b50c32d: Oh, I'll be fine. I've got work as Port Sarim's first lifeguard!
textByHashes.ec9253481b29730d4348529fe567aca1b50473d3: Wow! You sure are lucky! Seems the Lady Lumbridge just washed right up into the dock by herself!
textByHashes.eef042a82a5d2b72ff02223dafbc84b90ba976c3: No, it's not. It may, to the untrained eye, at first appear to be the Lady Lumbridge, but it is definitely not. It's my new ship. It just happens to look slightly similar, is all.
textByHashes.d2825503225466a883daff0734c668b132f57bba: Nope, you're mistaken. It's my new boat.
textByHashes.b884da6d2bd9054cbaea81ccfed9f4dfc27a3f49: Nope. I don't take passengers on my ship.
